title: Phase 2: Runner and CLI docs
description: Update documentation to reflect config-driven runner instantiation and backend resolution order. Document per-agent-type tool allowlisting for Claude Code backend.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md documents backend resolution order (flag > env > config)
Documentation is consistent with existing style

handoff_summary.completed: CLAUDE.md now documents: (1) backend resolution order at line 51 and Config wiring section, (2) per-agent-type tool allowlisting with default tools list and extras table, (3) how to override defaults via config.yaml.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. All three acceptance criteria are satisfied.
handoff_summary.risks: If default_config() tool lists change in the future, the CLAUDE.md table will need a corresponding update.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: CLAUDE.md is shared documentation; other doc beads editing this file concurrently could conflict.

conflict_risks: CLAUDE.md is shared documentation; other doc beads editing this file concurrently could conflict.

execution_history.summary: Added per-agent-type tool allowlisting table to CLAUDE.md under Multi-Backend Support. The backend resolution order (flag > env > config) was already documented. New content lists the 10 default Claude Code tools shared by all agent types and the extra tools granted to each agent type (developer, tester, documentation, planner, review), matching the defaults in config.py default_config().

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
